---
title: "analysis"
output: html_document
date: "2024-12-27"
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)

path = "C:/Users/USER/Documents/empirical_evidence_corren"

# Set the working directory for the whole document
# knitr::opts_knit$set(root.dir = path)
knitr::opts_knit$set(root.dir = here::here(path))
```

```{r include=FALSE}
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)
library(broom)
library(ggpubr)
library(tidyr)
library(gtable)
library(grid) # for unit.pmax()
library(gridExtra)
library(lubridate)
library(stargazer)
library(vtable)
library(stringr)
library(lmtest)
library(sandwich)
library(purrr)
library("readxl")
library(here)
library(haven)
library(scales)
library(fst)
library(data.table)

```


```{r include=FALSE}

load(file=paste(here(),"data", "df_withempirical.RData",sep = '/'))
```


```{r include=FALSE}


# Keep an untouched copy for comparison
df0 <- df

# Convert WITHOUT modifying df0
dt <- as.data.table(df0)

library(data.table)

# dt is already a data.table from your test
dt[, packageId := sub("\\.txt$", "", file_name)]
dt[, packageId := toupper(packageId)]  # normalize to match hearings file if needed

# Check
dt[1:10, .(file_name, packageId)]
dt[, .(n=.N, n_pkg=sum(!is.na(packageId)), pct=round(100*mean(!is.na(packageId)), 3))]

hearings <- fread(paste(here(),"data", "govinfo_house_and_joint_hearings.csv",sep = '/'))

# normalize packageId case on both sides
hearings[, packageId := toupper(packageId)]

# ensure year exists
hearings[, year := as.integer(substr(dateIssued, 1, 4))]

hearings <- unique(hearings[, .(packageId, title, congress, chamber, dateIssued, year)], by="packageId")

setkey(hearings, packageId)
setkey(dt, packageId)

out <- hearings[dt]  # left join, keeps all rows from dt

cat("Rows:", nrow(out), "\n")
cat("Matched (non-missing title):", sum(!is.na(out$title)), "\n")
cat("Unmatched:", sum(is.na(out$title)), "\n")


# If you named your joined table `out` and the pre-join df is `dt`
out[is.na(title), .(
  n = .N,
  n_file_name_na = sum(is.na(file_name)),
  n_packageId_na = sum(is.na(packageId))
)]

out[is.na(title) & !is.na(file_name),
    head(.SD, 30),
    .SDcols = c("file_name", "packageId")]

# classify unmatched by whether the ID looks like HHRG/JHRG/SHRG/etc
out[is.na(title) & !is.na(packageId), .(
  is_hhrg = sum(grepl("HHRG", packageId, ignore.case=TRUE)),
  is_jhrg = sum(grepl("JHRG", packageId, ignore.case=TRUE)),
  is_shrg = sum(grepl("SHRG", packageId, ignore.case=TRUE)),
  other   = sum(!grepl("(HHRG|JHRG|SHRG)", packageId, ignore.case=TRUE))
)]


out[is.na(title) & !is.na(file_name),
    head(.SD, 30),
    .SDcols = c("file_name", "packageId")]


head(out,100)

out$year



out %>%
  filter(!is.na(title)) %>%
  head(100)



write_fst(out, paste(here(),"data","out_hearings_enriched.fst",sep = '/'), compress = 100)



summary(out$bill)

sum(is.na(out$demratio))/nrow(out)*100

out %>%
  select(bill,title,title_speech,title_hearings,year,year,year_speech,congress_speech,congress_hearings) %>%
  filter(is.na(bill)) %>%
  head(1000)
```


```{r include=FALSE}





sum(is.na(df$year))/nrow(df)*100
sum(is.na(df$year_speech))/nrow(df)*100
sum(is.na(df$mean_score))/nrow(df)*100
sum(is.na(df$title_hearings))/nrow(df)*100
sum(is.na(df$title_speech))/nrow(df)*100


length(unique(df$title_speech))

df %>%
  filter(is.na(year_speech)) %>%
  select(speech,year_speech,year,title_speech,title_hearings,mean_score) %>%
  head(100)



title_level_years <- df %>%
  filter(!is.na(title_speech)) %>%
  group_by(title_speech) %>%
  summarise(
    year_agg = case_when(
      any(is.na(year)) ~ NA_integer_,
      n_distinct(year) == 1 ~ first(year),
      TRUE ~ NA_integer_   # conflicting years → treat as NA
    ),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

num_titles_with_na <- title_level_years %>%
  summarise(
    n_titles_na = sum(is.na(year_agg)),
    n_titles_total = n(),
    na_rate = n_titles_na / n_titles_total
  )

num_titles_with_na

titles_with_year <- title_level_years %>%
  filter(!is.na(year_agg))


titles_per_year <- titles_with_year %>%
  count(year_agg, name = "n_unique_titles") %>%
  arrange(year_agg)

titles_per_year





# 1) Hearings: simple numeric year
hearings_stats <- df %>%
  group_by(year) %>%
  summarise(
    total_rows     = n(),
    unique_titles  = n_distinct(title_hearings, na.rm = TRUE),
    missing_titles = sum(is.na(title_hearings)),
    missing_rate   = missing_titles / total_rows,
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  transmute(
    type = "hearings",
    year_group = as.character(year),  # <- key fix
    total_rows, unique_titles, missing_titles, missing_rate
  )

speech_stats <- df %>%
  mutate(
    year_group = str_squish(year_speech),
    year_group = na_if(year_group, "")
  ) %>%
  group_by(year_group) %>%
  summarise(
    total_rows     = n(),
    unique_titles  = n_distinct(title_speech, na.rm = TRUE),
    missing_titles = sum(is.na(title_speech)),
    missing_rate   = missing_titles / total_rows,
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  mutate(type = "speech") %>%
  select(type, year_group, total_rows, unique_titles, missing_titles, missing_rate)

summary_bins <- bind_rows(hearings_stats, speech_stats) %>%
  arrange(type, year_group)


summary_bins


unique(df$year_speech)
```



```{r include=FALSE}

out <- read_fst(paste(here(),"data","out_hearings_enriched.fst",sep = '/'))



df <- out

unique_years <- sort(unique(na.omit(df$year)), decreasing = FALSE)
unique_congress <- sort(unique(na.omit(df$congress)), decreasing = FALSE)

custom_ticks <- unique_years[seq(1, length(unique_years), by = 2)]  # Select every 2nd year
custom_ticks <- seq(1996, 2016, by = 2)


# Define Congress sessions and their corresponding years
congress_mapping <- data.frame(
  congress = unique_congress,
  start_year = seq(1997, by = 2, length.out = 10),
  end_year = seq(1998, by = 2, length.out = 10)
)

# Generate custom ticks for the x-axis
custom_labels <- paste0(custom_ticks, " (", df$congress[match(custom_ticks, df$year)],"th)")



#write.csv(sample, paste(path,"data", "sample.csv",sep = '/'), row.names = FALSE)

#df_1 <- read.csv(paste(here(),"data", "merged_metadata_df.csv",sep = '/'))



```
# descriptive table
```{r include=FALSE}

library(dplyr)
library(knitr)
library(kableExtra)

# df = your dataset
# expected columns: year, congress, title, speech

# Main table
tab <- df %>%
  mutate(
    year = as.integer(year),
    congress = as.integer(congress)
  ) %>%
  group_by(year, congress) %>%
  summarise(
    hearings = n_distinct(packageId),
    speeches = n_distinct(speech),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  arrange(year, congress) %>%
  filter(hearings > 3) %>%
  filter(!is.na(year), !is.na(congress)) %>%   # <-- DROP NA ROWS
  mutate(
    year = as.character(year),
    congress = as.character(congress)
  )

totals <- tab %>%
  summarise(
    year = "Total",
    congress = "",
    hearings = sum(hearings),
    speeches = sum(speeches)
  )

tab_final <- bind_rows(tab, totals)

latex_table <- knitr::kable(
  tab_final,
  format = "latex",
  booktabs = TRUE,
  col.names = c("Year", "Congress", "Unique Hearings", "Unique Speech Chunks"),
  caption = "Descriptive statistics by year and Congress."
)

file = paste(here(),"plots", "descriptive_stats_hearings.tex",sep = '/')

writeLines(latex_table, file)


```

```{r include=FALSE}

df_new_parsing <- read.csv(paste(here(),"hearings_testing", "sentence_predictions_all.csv",sep = '/'))

head(df_new_parsing,100)


df_new_parsing %>%
  summarise(
    n_unique_title_hearings = n_distinct(title_hearings),
    .groups = "drop"
  )
```


```{r include=FALSE}
a <- df %>%
  group_by(hearing) %>%
  summarise(mean_empirical = mean(mean_score)) 

mean_dist = mean(a$mean_empirical)
median_dist = median(a$mean_empirical)

mean_dist
density <- df %>%
  group_by(hearing) %>%
  summarise(mean_empirical = mean(mean_score)) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x=mean_empirical)) +
  geom_density() +
  geom_vline(aes(xintercept = mean(mean_empirical)), 
             linetype = "dashed", color = "blue", size = 1)+
  labs(
    x = "Empirical Score",
    y = "Density") +
  theme_minimal()

df

density
# Save the plot
ggsave(paste(path,"plots","empirical_density.png",sep = "/"),
  density, width = 8, height = 6, dpi = 300)



pkg_level <- df %>%
  group_by(packageId) %>%
  summarise(
    empirical_score = mean(mean_score, na.rm = TRUE)
  ) %>%
  ungroup()

stargazer(
  as.data.frame(pkg_level["empirical_score"]),   # 1-col data.frame (keeps stargazer in "summary" mode)
  type = "latex",
  summary = TRUE,
  digits = 3,
  covariate.labels = "Empirical Score (Package-Level Mean)",
  title = "Descriptive Statistics of Package-Level Empirical Score",
  label = "tab:desc_empirical_score_pkg",
  summary.stat = c("n", "mean", "sd", "median", "min", "max")
)
```


```{r include=FALSE}
# Define the variables to plot
class_const_vars <- paste0("class_const_", 1:5)

# Loop through each variable to create and save plots
for (var in class_const_vars) {
  const_plot <- df %>%
    filter(!is.na(.data[[var]])) %>%  # Dynamically filter non-NA values for the current variable
    mutate(current_var = as.factor(.data[[var]])) %>%  # Dynamically create a factor for the current variable
    group_by(hearing, current_var, year) %>%
    summarise(mean_empirical = mean(mean_score), .groups = "drop") %>%
    group_by(current_var, year) %>%
    summarise(empirical = mean(mean_empirical), .groups = "drop") %>%
    ggplot(aes(y = empirical, x = year, group = current_var, color = current_var, linetype = current_var)) +
    geom_point(aes(shape = current_var)) +  # Different shapes for points
    geom_smooth(se = FALSE) +  # Remove SE from smooth lines
    scale_x_continuous(breaks = custom_ticks) +  # Tick marks for years
    scale_color_manual(values = c("1" = "red", "0" = "black"),  # Different colors
                       labels = c("1" = "Constitutional", "0" = "Non-Constitutional")) +
    scale_linetype_manual(values = c("1" = "dashed", "0" = "solid"),  # Different line types
                          labels = c("1" = "Constitutional", "0" = "Non-Constitutional")) +
    scale_shape_manual(values = c("1" = 17, "0" = 16),  # Different shapes (circle and triangle)
                       labels = c("1" = "Constitutional", "0" = "Non-Constitutional")) +
    labs(
      x = "Year",
      y = "Empirical Score",
      title = paste("Empirical Score by Year for", var)
    ) +
    theme_minimal() +
    theme(
      legend.title = element_blank(),  # Remove legend title
      legend.position = "right"       # Adjust legend position if necessary
    )

  # Save each plot with a unique filename
  ggsave(
    filename = paste0(path, "/plots/", var, "_plot.png"),
    plot = const_plot,
    width = 8,
    height = 6,
    dpi = 300
  )
}



# regression coefs


coefficients_list <- list()

# Loop through each variable and fit the model
for (var in class_const_vars) {
  # Fit the model
  model <- lm(
    formula = as.formula(paste(
      "mean_score ~ year + congress_hearings + pres_party + house_maj +",
      "divided_house + divided_senate + dem_dw + median_floor + chair_dw +",
      "dem + type_legislative + bill +", var, "+ cmt_govtrack + majortopic2"
    )),
    data = df
  )
  
  # Check if the variable is in the model
  if (var %in% rownames(coef(summary(model)))) {
    # Extract coefficient and confidence intervals
    coef <- coef(summary(model))[var, "Estimate"]
    ci <- confint(model)[var, ]
    
    # Save the results
    coefficients_list[[var]] <- list(
      coefficient = coef,
      ci_lower = ci[1],
      ci_upper = ci[2]
    )
  } else {
    # Variable not in the model
    coefficients_list[[var]] <- list(
      coefficient = NA,
      ci_lower = NA,
      ci_upper = NA
    )
    warning(paste("Variable", var, "was not included in the model."))
  }
}


# Define custom labels for the variables
custom_labels <- c(
  "class_const_1" = "Minimal",
  "class_const_2" = "Textual",
  "class_const_3" = "Extended Textual",
  "class_const_4" = "Originalism",
  "class_const_5" = "Expansive"
)

# Transform the coefficients list into a data frame
coefficients_df <- do.call(rbind, lapply(names(coefficients_list), function(var) {
  data.frame(
    variable = var,
    coefficient = coefficients_list[[var]]$coefficient,
    ci_lower = coefficients_list[[var]]$ci_lower,
    ci_upper = coefficients_list[[var]]$ci_upper
  )
})) %>%
  mutate(
    label = factor(custom_labels[variable], levels = custom_labels)  # Maintain custom order
  )



# Plot the coefficients
ggplot(coefficients_df, aes(x = coefficient, y = label)) +
  geom_point(size = 3) +  # Plot points for coefficients
  geom_errorbarh(aes(xmin = ci_lower, xmax = ci_upper), height = 0.2) +  # Add horizontal error bars for CIs
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "red") +  # Vertical line at 0
  labs(
    x = "Coefficient",
    y = NULL,  # Y-axis label removed to use variable names directly
    title = "Regression Coefficients for Con Dicts"
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 10),  # Customize y-axis text size
    axis.title.x = element_text(size = 12) # Customize x-axis title size
  )



##############################
##############################
##############################

# using continuous variables

class_const_vars <- paste0("average_freq_ver", 1:5)


coefficients_list <- list()

# Loop through each variable and fit the model
for (var in class_const_vars) {
  # Fit the model
  model <- lm(
    formula = as.formula(paste(
      "mean_score ~ year + congress_hearings + pres_party + house_maj +",
      "divided_house + divided_senate + dem_dw + median_floor + chair_dw +",
      "dem + type_legislative + bill +", var, "+ cmt_govtrack + majortopic2"
    )),
    data = df
  )
  
  # Check if the variable is in the model
  if (var %in% rownames(coef(summary(model)))) {
    # Extract coefficient and confidence intervals
    coef <- coef(summary(model))[var, "Estimate"]
    ci <- confint(model)[var, ]
    
    # Save the results
    coefficients_list[[var]] <- list(
      coefficient = coef,
      ci_lower = ci[1],
      ci_upper = ci[2]
    )
  } else {
    # Variable not in the model
    coefficients_list[[var]] <- list(
      coefficient = NA,
      ci_lower = NA,
      ci_upper = NA
    )
    warning(paste("Variable", var, "was not included in the model."))
  }
}


# Define custom labels for the variables
custom_labels <- c(
  "average_freq_ver1" = "Minimal",
  "average_freq_ver2" = "Textual",
  "average_freq_ver3" = "Extended Textual",
  "average_freq_ver4" = "Originalism",
  "average_freq_ver5" = "Expansive"
)

# Transform the coefficients list into a data frame
coefficients_df <- do.call(rbind, lapply(names(coefficients_list), function(var) {
  data.frame(
    variable = var,
    coefficient = coefficients_list[[var]]$coefficient,
    ci_lower = coefficients_list[[var]]$ci_lower,
    ci_upper = coefficients_list[[var]]$ci_upper
  )
})) %>%
  mutate(
    label = factor(custom_labels[variable], levels = custom_labels)  # Maintain custom order
  )



# Plot the coefficients
ggplot(coefficients_df, aes(x = coefficient, y = label)) +
  geom_point(size = 3) +  # Plot points for coefficients
  geom_errorbarh(aes(xmin = ci_lower, xmax = ci_upper), height = 0.2) +  # Add horizontal error bars for CIs
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "red") +  # Vertical line at 0
  labs(
    x = "Coefficient",
    y = NULL,  # Y-axis label removed to use variable names directly
    title = "Regression Coefficients for Con Dicts"
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 10),  # Customize y-axis text size
    axis.title.x = element_text(size = 12) # Customize x-axis title size
  )
```



# main plots
```{r include=FALSE}

party_control <- read.csv(paste(here(),"data", "congresS_control.csv",sep = '/'))

party_control <- party_control %>%
  filter(end_year<= 2017)


time_plot <- df %>%
  filter(!is.na(year)) %>%
  group_by(hearing,year) %>%
  summarise(mean_empirical = mean(mean_score)) %>%
  group_by(year) %>%
  summarise(empirical = mean(mean_empirical)) %>%
  ggplot(aes(y=empirical,x=year))+
  geom_point()+
  geom_smooth()+
   scale_x_continuous(
    breaks = custom_ticks)+  # Tick marks for years  
  labs(
    x = "Year",
    y = "Empirical Score") +
  theme_minimal()

ggsave(paste(here(),"plots","time_plot.png",sep = "/"),
  time_plot, width = 8, height = 6, dpi = 300)

time_plot




minority_plot <- df %>%
  filter(!is.na(minority)) %>%
  filter(!is.na(majority_party)) %>%
  mutate(
    minority = as.factor(if_else(minority == 1, "Minority", "Majority"))  # Replace 1 with Minority, 0 with Majority
  ) %>%
  group_by(hearing, minority, year) %>%
  summarise(mean_empirical = mean(mean_score, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop") %>%
  group_by(minority, year) %>%
  summarise(empirical = mean(mean_empirical, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop") %>%
  ggplot(aes(y = empirical, x = year, group = minority)) +
  geom_point(aes(x = year + 0.5, shape = minority), size = 2) +  # Shift points leftward by 0.5
  geom_smooth(aes(x = year + 0.5,linetype = minority), color = "black", se = TRUE) +  # Black lines with SE and line types
  geom_rect(
    data = party_control,  # Use broader ranges for shading
    aes(
      xmin = start_year, xmax = end_year,  # Use start and end year for ranges
      ymin = -Inf, ymax = Inf, 
      fill = majority_party
    ),
    inherit.aes = FALSE,
    alpha = 0.2  # Transparency for shading
  ) +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = custom_ticks) +  # Custom tick marks for years
  scale_shape_manual(
    values = c("Minority" = 17, "Majority" = 16),  # Custom shapes for points
    name = "Committee Control"
  ) +
  scale_linetype_manual(
    values = c("Minority" = "dashed", "Majority" = "solid"),  # Different line types
    name = "Committee Control"
  ) +
  scale_fill_manual(
    values = c("D" = "blue", "R" = "red"),  # Shading for Majority party
    name = "House Control"  # Legend title for shading
  ) +
  labs(
    x = "Year",
    y = "Empirical Score"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()


ggsave(
    filename = paste0(path, "/plots/minority_plot.png"),
    plot = minority_plot,
    width = 8,
    height = 6,
    dpi = 300
  )


party_plot <- df %>%
  filter(!is.na(dem)) %>%
  mutate(
    dem = as.factor(if_else(dem == 1, "D", "R"))  # Replace 1 with D and 0 with R
  ) %>%
  group_by(hearing, dem, year) %>%
  summarise(mean_empirical = mean(mean_score, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop") %>%
  group_by(dem, year) %>%
  summarise(empirical = mean(mean_empirical, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop") %>%
  ggplot(aes(y = empirical, x = year, group = dem, color = dem)) +
  # geom_rect(
  #   data = party_control,  # Use the updated party_control dataset
  #   aes(
  #     xmin = start_year, xmax = end_year,  # Use start and end years for ranges
  #     ymin = -Inf, ymax = Inf, 
  #     fill = majority_party
  #   ),
  #   inherit.aes = FALSE,
  #   alpha = 0.2  # Transparency for shading
  # ) +
  geom_rect(
    data = party_control,
    aes(
      xmin = start_year,
      xmax = end_year,
      ymin = -Inf,
      ymax = Inf,
      fill = majority_party,
      alpha = power_gap_scaled
    ),
    inherit.aes = FALSE
  ) +
  geom_point(aes(x = year + 0.5)) +  # Shift points leftward by 0.5
#  geom_smooth(aes(x = year + 0.5),se = TRUE) +  # Add SE to smooth lines
  geom_line(aes(x = year + 0.5)) +  # Replaces geom_smooth
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = custom_ticks) +  # Custom tick marks for years
  scale_color_manual(
    values = c("R" = "red", "D" = "blue"),  # Custom colors for R and D
    name = "Party"  # Legend title
  ) +
  scale_fill_manual(
    values = c("D" = "blue", "R" = "red"),  # Shading for Majority party
    name = "House Control"  # Legend title for shading
  ) +
  scale_alpha_continuous(range = c(0.1, 0.5)) + 
  guides(alpha = "none") +  
  labs(
    x = "Year",
    y = "Empirical Score"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()

party_plot


ggsave(
    filename = paste0(here(), "/plots/party_plot.png"),
    plot = party_plot,
    width = 8,
    height = 6,
    dpi = 300
  )


party_plot_facet <- df %>%
  filter(!is.na(dem)) %>%
  filter(!is.na(cmt_govtrack)) %>%
  mutate(
    dem = as.factor(if_else(dem == 1, "D", "R"))
  ) %>%
  group_by(cmt_govtrack, hearing, dem, year) %>%
  summarise(mean_empirical = mean(mean_score, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop") %>%
  group_by(cmt_govtrack, dem, year) %>%
  summarise(empirical = mean(mean_empirical, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop") %>%
  ggplot(aes(y = empirical, x = year, group = dem, color = dem)) +
  geom_rect(
    data = party_control,
    aes(
      xmin = start_year,
      xmax = end_year,
      ymin = -Inf,
      ymax = Inf,
      fill = majority_party,
      alpha = power_gap_scaled
    ),
    inherit.aes = FALSE
  ) +
  geom_point(aes(x = year + 0.5)) +
  geom_line(aes(x = year + 0.5)) +
  facet_wrap(~ cmt_govtrack) +
# scale_x_continuous(breaks = custom_ticks) +
  scale_color_manual(
    values = c("R" = "red", "D" = "blue"),
    name = "Party"
  ) +
  scale_fill_manual(
    values = c("D" = "blue", "R" = "red"),
    name = "House Control"
  ) +
  scale_alpha_continuous(range = c(0.1, 0.5)) +
  guides(alpha = "none") +
  labs(
    x = "Year",
    y = "Empirical Score"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()


ggsave(
    filename = paste0(path, "/plots/party_plot_facet.png"),
    plot = party_plot_facet,
    width = 8,
    height = 6,
    dpi = 300
  )



committees <- unique(na.omit(df$cmt_govtrack))

# Loop through each committee
for (committee in committees) {
  
  # Filter and summarize data for just this committee
  committee_df <- df %>%
    filter(!is.na(dem), cmt_govtrack == committee) %>%
    mutate(dem = as.factor(if_else(dem == 1, "D", "R"))) %>%
    group_by(hearing, dem, year) %>%
    summarise(mean_empirical = mean(mean_score, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop") %>%
    group_by(dem, year) %>%
    summarise(empirical = mean(mean_empirical, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop")
  
  # Create the plot
  p <- ggplot(committee_df, aes(y = empirical, x = year, group = dem, color = dem)) +
    geom_rect(
      data = party_control,
      aes(
        xmin = start_year,
        xmax = end_year,
        ymin = -Inf,
        ymax = Inf,
        fill = majority_party,
        alpha = power_gap_scaled
      ),
      inherit.aes = FALSE
    ) +
    geom_point(aes(x = year + 0.5)) +
    geom_line(aes(x = year + 0.5)) +
    scale_x_continuous(breaks = custom_ticks) +
    scale_color_manual(values = c("R" = "red", "D" = "blue")) +
    scale_fill_manual(values = c("D" = "blue", "R" = "red")) +
    scale_alpha_continuous(range = c(0.1, 0.5)) +
    guides(alpha = "none", fill = guide_legend(title = "House Control"), color = guide_legend(title = "Party")) +
    labs(
      title = paste("Empirical Score Over Time –", committee),
      x = "Year",
      y = "Empirical Score"
    ) +
    theme_minimal()
  
  
  file_path = paste0(path,"/plots/","party_plot_",committee, ".png")
  
  # Save the plot
  ggsave(filename = file_path,
         plot = p,
         width = 10, height = 6, dpi = 300)
}






library(forcats)

# 1) Build a stable ordered factor for bins (ordered by first year found)
bin_levels <- df %>%
  distinct(year_speech) %>%
  mutate(
    year_bin = str_squish(year_speech),
    first_year = suppressWarnings(as.integer(str_extract(year_bin, "\\d{4}")))
  ) %>%
  filter(!is.na(year_bin), year_bin != "") %>%
  arrange(first_year, year_bin) %>%
  pull(year_bin)

party_plot_with_year_speech <- df %>%
  filter(!is.na(dem)) %>%
  mutate(
    dem = as.factor(if_else(dem == 1, "D", "R")),
    year_bin = str_squish(year_speech),
    year_bin = na_if(year_bin, ""),
    year_bin = factor(year_bin, levels = bin_levels),
    x_pos = as.numeric(year_bin)   # numeric positions for shading if needed
  ) %>%
  filter(!is.na(year_bin)) %>%
  group_by(hearing, dem, year_bin, x_pos) %>%
  summarise(mean_empirical = mean(mean_score, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop") %>%
  group_by(dem, year_bin, x_pos) %>%
  summarise(empirical = mean(mean_empirical, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop") %>%
  ggplot(aes(y = empirical, x = year_bin, group = dem, color = dem)) +

  # rectangles: map numeric index; use an alternate aesthetic with inherit.aes = FALSE
  geom_rect(
    data = party_control %>%
      mutate(
        # map the numeric year range to bin-index space
        xmin = findInterval(start_year, sort(unique(bin_levels |> str_extract("\\d{4}") |> as.integer())), rightmost.closed = TRUE) + 0.5,
        xmax = findInterval(end_year,   sort(unique(bin_levels |> str_extract("\\d{4}") |> as.integer())), rightmost.closed = TRUE) + 1.5
      ),
    aes(
      xmin = xmin,
      xmax = xmax,
      ymin = -Inf,
      ymax = Inf,
      fill = majority_party,
      alpha = power_gap_scaled
    ),
    inherit.aes = FALSE
  ) +

  geom_point(position = position_nudge(x = -0.15)) +
  geom_line(position = position_nudge(x = -0.15)) +

  scale_x_discrete(drop = FALSE) +
  scale_color_manual(
    values = c("R" = "red", "D" = "blue"),
    name = "Party"
  ) +
  scale_fill_manual(
    values = c("D" = "blue", "R" = "red"),
    name = "House Control"
  ) +
  scale_alpha_continuous(range = c(0.1, 0.5)) +
  guides(alpha = "none") +
  labs(x = "Year bin (speech)", y = "Empirical Score") +
  theme_minimal()


party_plot_with_year_speech

ggsave(
    filename = paste0(here(), "/plots/party_plot_with_year_speech.png"),
    plot = party_plot_with_year_speech,
    width = 8,
    height = 6,
    dpi = 300
  )


```



```{r include=FALSE}

specific_bill <- df %>%
  filter(!is.na(bill)) %>%
  mutate(bill = as.factor(bill)) %>%
  group_by(hearing,bill,year) %>%
  summarise(mean_empirical = mean(mean_score)) %>%
  group_by(bill,year) %>%
  summarise(empirical = mean(mean_empirical)) %>%
  ggplot(aes(y=empirical,x=year,group=bill,color=bill))+
  geom_point()+
  geom_smooth(se = TRUE) +  # Remove SE from smooth lines
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = custom_ticks) +  # Custom tick marks for years
  scale_color_manual(values = c("1" = "blue", "0" = "black"),
                     labels = c("1" = "Bill on Table", "0" = "No Bill")) +
  labs(
    x = "Year",
    y = "Empirical Score",
    color = "Hearing Type"
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    legend.title = element_blank(),
    legend.position = "right"       # Adjust legend position if necessary
  )


specific_bill

# Save the plot
ggsave(paste(here(),"plots","specific_bill_plot.png",sep = "/"),
  specific_bill, width = 8, height = 6, dpi = 300)



type_hearing <- df %>%
  filter(!is.na(type)) %>%
  mutate(type = as.factor(type)) %>%
  group_by(hearing,type,year) %>%
  summarise(mean_empirical = mean(mean_score)) %>%
  group_by(type,year) %>%
  summarise(empirical = mean(mean_empirical)) %>%
  ggplot(aes(y=empirical,x=year,group=type,color=type))+
  geom_point()+
  geom_smooth(se = TRUE) +  # Remove SE from smooth lines
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = custom_ticks) +  # Custom tick marks for years
  scale_color_manual(values = c("oversight" = "blue", "legislative" = "black"),
                     labels = c("oversight" = "Oversight", "legislative" = "Legislative")) +
  labs(
    x = "Year",
    y = "Empirical Score",
    color = "Hearing Type"
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    legend.position = "right"       # Adjust legend position if necessary
  )

type_hearing
# Save the plot
ggsave(paste(here(),"plots","type_hearing.png",sep = "/"),
  type_hearing, width = 8, height = 6, dpi = 300)




# by committee

committee_empirical_bar_chart <- df %>%
  filter(!is.na(committee_code2)) %>%
  group_by(committee_code2) %>%
  summarise(
    mean_empirical = mean(mean_score),
    sd_empirical = sd(mean_score),  # Standard deviation
    n = n()  # Sample size
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    se_empirical = sd_empirical / sqrt(n)  # Standard error
  ) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = reorder(committee_code2, mean_empirical), y = mean_empirical)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", fill = "skyblue", width = 0.7) +  # Bar chart
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = mean_empirical - se_empirical, ymax = mean_empirical + se_empirical), 
                width = 0.2) +  # Add error bars
  labs(
    x = "Committee",
    y = "Mean Empirical Score") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1)  # Rotate x-axis labels for better readability
  )


# Save the plot
ggsave(paste(path,"plots","committee_empirical_bar_chart.png",sep = "/"),
  committee_empirical_bar_chart, width = 8, height = 6, dpi = 300)



committee_empirical_bar_chart_2 <- df %>%
  filter(!is.na(cmt_govtrack)) %>%
  group_by(cmt_govtrack) %>%
  summarise(
    mean_empirical = mean(mean_score),
    sd_empirical = sd(mean_score),  # Standard deviation
    n = n()  # Sample size
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    se_empirical = sd_empirical / sqrt(n)  # Standard error
  ) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = reorder(cmt_govtrack, mean_empirical), y = mean_empirical)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", fill = "skyblue", width = 0.7) +  # Bar chart
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = mean_empirical - se_empirical, ymax = mean_empirical + se_empirical), 
                width = 0.2) +  # Add error bars
  labs(
    x = "Committee",
    y = "Mean Empirical Score") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1)  # Rotate x-axis labels for better readability
  )

# Save the plot
ggsave(paste(path,"plots","committee_empirical_bar_chart_2.png",sep = "/"),
  committee_empirical_bar_chart_2, width = 8, height = 6, dpi = 300)



## using extended textual dict
committee_empirical_bar_withconst <- df %>%
  filter(!is.na(class_const_3)) %>%
  mutate(
    class_const_label = ifelse(class_const_3 == 1, "Constitutional", "Non-Constitutional")  # Map values to labels
  ) %>%
  filter(!is.na(cmt_govtrack)) %>%
  group_by(cmt_govtrack, class_const_label) %>%
  summarise(
    mean_empirical = mean(mean_score),
    count = n(),
    .groups = 'drop'
  ) %>%
  group_by(cmt_govtrack) %>%
  mutate(
    total_count = sum(count),
    prop_class_const = count / total_count  # Proportion of class_const
  ) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  ggplot(aes(
    x = reorder(cmt_govtrack, mean_empirical), 
    y = mean_empirical, 
    fill = class_const_label
  )) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", position = "stack", width = 0.7) +  # Stacked bar chart
  labs(
    x = "Committee",
    y = "Mean Empirical Score"
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),  # Rotate x-axis labels
    legend.title = element_blank()  # Remove legend title
  )

ggsave(paste(path,"plots","committee_empirical_bar_withconst.png",sep = "/"),
  committee_empirical_bar_withconst, width = 8, height = 6, dpi = 300)


topic_bar_chart <- df %>%
  filter(!is.na(majortopic2) & majortopic2 != "") %>%
  group_by(majortopic2) %>%
  summarise(
    mean_empirical = mean(mean_score),
    sd_empirical = sd(mean_score),  # Standard deviation
    n = n()  # Sample size
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    se_empirical = sd_empirical / sqrt(n)  # Standard error
  ) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = reorder(majortopic2, mean_empirical), y = mean_empirical)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", fill = "orange", width = 0.7) +  # Bar chart
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = mean_empirical - se_empirical, ymax = mean_empirical + se_empirical), 
                width = 0.2) +  # Add error bars
  labs(
    x = "Hearing Topic",
    y = "Mean Empirical Score") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1)  # Rotate x-axis labels for better readability
  )

# Save the plot
ggsave(paste(path,"plots","topic_bar_chart.png",sep = "/"),
  topic_bar_chart, width = 8, height = 6, dpi = 300)

unique(df$year)

names_dist <- df %>%
  filter(!is.na(thomas_name) & thomas_name != "") %>%
    group_by(thomas_name, dem) %>%
  summarise(
    mean_empirical = mean(mean_score, na.rm = TRUE),
    sd_empirical = sd(mean_score, na.rm = TRUE),
    n = n(),  # Number of observations per group
    se_empirical = sd_empirical / sqrt(n),  # Standard error
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = mean_empirical, fill = as.factor(dem))) +
  geom_density(alpha = 0.5) +  # Density plot with transparency
  scale_fill_manual(
    values = c("0" = "red", "1" = "blue"),
    labels = c("0" = "R", "1" = "D"),
    name = "Party"
  ) +
  labs(
    x = "Mean Empirical Score",
    y = "Density") +
  theme_minimal()

# Save the plot
ggsave(paste(path,"plots","names_dist.png",sep = "/"),
  names_dist, width = 8, height = 6, dpi = 300)


cmt_overtime <- df %>%
    mutate(
    cmt_govtrack = gsub("HOUSE COMMITTEE ON ", "", cmt_govtrack, fixed = TRUE),
    cmt_govtrack = tools::toTitleCase(tolower(cmt_govtrack))
  ) %>%
  filter(!is.na(cmt_govtrack)) %>%
  group_by(cmt_govtrack, year) %>%
  summarise(empirical = mean(mean_score, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop") %>%
  ggplot(aes(y = empirical, x = year, group = cmt_govtrack, color = cmt_govtrack)) +
  geom_smooth(se = FALSE) +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = custom_ticks) +  # Custom tick marks for years
  labs(
    x = "Year",
    y = "Empirical Score"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()

ggsave(paste(path,"plots","cmt_overtime.png",sep = "/"),
  cmt_overtime, width = 8, height = 6, dpi = 300)




```


```{r message=FALSE, warning=FALSE, include=FALSE, paged.print=FALSE}
library(stargazer)


# speech as unit of analysis


mode_nonmissing <- function(x) {
  x <- x[!is.na(x)]
  if (length(x) == 0) return(NA_integer_)
  ux <- unique(x)
  ux[which.max(tabulate(match(x, ux)))]
}

df$cmt_govtrack

df_speech <- df %>%
  group_by(speech) %>%
  summarise(
    mean_score   = mean(mean_score, na.rm = TRUE),
    dem          = first(na.omit(dem)),
    house_maj    = first(na.omit(house_maj)),
    minority     = first(na.omit(minority)),
    seniority_rs = first(na.omit(seniority_rs)),
    cmt_govtrack = first(na.omit(cmt_govtrack)),
    majortopic2 = first(na.omit(majortopic2)),
    thomas_name = first(na.omit(thomas_name)),
    year         = mode_nonmissing(as.integer(year)),
    # Optional diagnostics:
    year_nunique = n_distinct(year[!is.na(year)]),
    year_any_na  = any(is.na(year)),
    .groups = "drop"
  )


df_speech_full <- df_speech %>%
  filter(!is.na(year)) %>%
  filter(!is.na(seniority_rs)) %>%
  filter(!is.na(minority))


df_speech_full$house_maj_d <- ifelse(
  df_speech_full$house_maj == "D", 1,
  ifelse(df_speech_full$house_maj == "R", 0, NA)
)


# M1: Party + House Control + Year FE
m1 <- lm(
  mean_score ~ dem + house_maj_d  + factor(year),
  data = df_speech_full
)

# M2: + Minority Status
m2 <- lm(
  mean_score ~ dem + house_maj_d  + minority + factor(year),
  data = df_speech_full
)

# M3: + Seniority + Seniority × Minority
m3 <- lm(
  mean_score ~ dem + house_maj_d  + minority +
    seniority_rs + minority:seniority_rs +
    factor(year),
  data = df_speech_full
)

# M4: + Committee FE + Topic FE
m4 <- lm(
  mean_score ~ dem + house_maj_d  + minority +
    seniority_rs + minority:seniority_rs +
    factor(year) + factor(cmt_govtrack) + factor(majortopic2),
  data = df_speech_full
)

# M5: + Individual Member FE
m5 <- lm(
  mean_score ~ dem + house_maj_d  + minority +
    seniority_rs + minority:seniority_rs +
    factor(year) + factor(cmt_govtrack) +
    factor(majortopic2) + factor(thomas_name),
  data = df_speech_full
)


# --------- Prepare: names/labels you want in the table ----------




library(stargazer)

# Models list (you already have this)
models <- list(m1, m2, m3, m4, m5)

# Coefficients you want to show
keep_vars <- c(
  "dem",
  "house_maj_d",
  "minority",
  "seniority_rs",
  "minority:seniority_rs"
)

# Regex to omit all fixed effects
omit_fe <- c(
  "factor\\(year\\)",
  "factor\\(cmt_govtrack\\)",
  "factor\\(majortopic2\\)",
  "factor\\(thomas_name\\)"
)

year_fe      <- c("Yes", "Yes", "Yes", "Yes", "Yes")
committee_fe <- c("No",  "No",  "No",  "Yes", "Yes")
topic_fe     <- c("No",  "No",  "No",  "Yes", "Yes")
member_fe    <- c("No",  "No",  "No",  "No",  "Yes")



stargazer(
  models,
  type = "latex",        # change to "latex" or "html" as needed
  keep = keep_vars,
  omit = omit_fe,
  covariate.labels = row_labels[-1],  # drop intercept label
  omit.stat = c("f", "ser"),
  dep.var.labels = "Mean Speech Score",
  column.labels = c("M1", "M2", "M3", "M4", "M5"),
  add.lines = list(
    c("Year Fixed Effects", year_fe),
    c("Committee Fixed Effects", committee_fe),
    c("Topic Fixed Effects", topic_fe),
    c("Member Fixed Effects", member_fe)
  ),
  no.space = TRUE
)

# keep_order <- c(
#   "(Intercept)",
#   "dem",
#   "house_maj_d",
#   "minority",
#   "seniority_rs",
#   "minority:seniority_rs"
# )
# 
# cov_labels <- c(
#   "Intercept",
#   "Party (D)",
#   "House Control (D)",
#   "Minority Status",
#   "Seniority",
#   "Seniority × Minority"
# )
# 
# # --- Create stargazer output (capture to wrap in resizebox) ---
# starg_out <- capture.output(
#   stargazer(
#     m1, m2, m3, m4, m5,
#     type = "latex",
#     dep.var.labels = "Empirical Score",
#     title = "Regression Results",
#     label = "tab:main_regressions",
#     align = TRUE,
#     no.space = TRUE,
#     digits = 3,
#     star.cutoffs = c(0.05, 0.01, 0.001),
# 
#     keep = keep_order,
#     order = keep_order,
#     covariate.labels = cov_labels,
# 
#     # Omit FE coefficients but KEEP intercept (make sure the FE names match your models)
#     omit = c(
#       "factor\\(year\\)",
#       "factor\\(cmt_govtrack\\)",
#       "factor\\(majortopic2\\)",
#       "factor\\(thomas_name\\)",   # your model used factor(thomas_name)
#       "factor\\(member_id\\)"      # keep this too if sometimes used
#     ),
# 
#     # Remove default wide summary rows (we add our own)
#     omit.stat = c("f", "ser", "n", "rsq", "adj.rsq"),
# 
#     # add custom lines
#     add.lines = list(
#       c("Year fixed effects", "Yes", "Yes", "Yes", "Yes", "Yes"),
#       c("Committee fixed effects", "No", "No", "No", "Yes", "Yes"),
#       c("Topic fixed effects", "No", "No", "No", "Yes", "Yes"),
#       c("Member fixed effects", "No", "No", "No", "No", "Yes"),
#       c("Observations",
#         format(nobs(m1), big.mark = ","),
#         format(nobs(m2), big.mark = ","),
#         format(nobs(m3), big.mark = ","),
#         format(nobs(m4), big.mark = ","),
#         format(nobs(m5), big.mark = ",")),
#       c("$R^2$",
#         sprintf("%.3f", summary(m1)$r.squared),
#         sprintf("%.3f", summary(m2)$r.squared),
#         sprintf("%.3f", summary(m3)$r.squared),
#         sprintf("%.3f", summary(m4)$r.squared),
#         sprintf("%.3f", summary(m5)$r.squared)),
#       c("Adj. $R^2$",
#         sprintf("%.3f", summary(m1)$adj.r.squared),
#         sprintf("%.3f", summary(m2)$adj.r.squared),
#         sprintf("%.3f", summary(m3)$adj.r.squared),
#         sprintf("%.3f", summary(m4)$adj.r.squared),
#         sprintf("%.3f", summary(m5)$adj.r.squared))
#     ),
# 
#     notes = c(
#       "The unit of analysis is the speech chunk.",
#       "All models include year fixed effects.",
#       "Committee, topic, and individual member fixed effects are included where indicated.",
#       "OLS estimates with standard errors in parentheses."
#     ),
#     notes.align = "l",
# 
#     out = NULL   # capture output instead of writing directly
#   )
# )
# 
# texfile <- file.path(here(), "plots", "main_regression_table.tex")
# 
# # Header + resize open
# header <- c(
#   "\\begin{table}[!htbp]",
#   "\\centering",
#   "\\caption{Regression Results}",
#   "\\label{tab:main_regressions}",
#   "\\resizebox{\\textwidth}{!}{%"
# )
# 
# # Footer + resize close
# footer <- c(
#   "}", # close resizebox
#   "\\end{table}"
# )
# 
# # Combine and write
# writeLines(c(header, starg_out, footer), con = texfile)
# 
# cat("Wrote LaTeX table to:", texfile, "\n")
# 
# 
# 
# # show which coefficients were aliased/dropped in M5
# alias(m5)
# 
# # check for NA coefficients (dropped)
# coefs_m5 <- coef(summary(m5))
# coefs_m5[!complete.cases(coefs_m5), , drop = FALSE]
# 
# rownames(coef(summary(m3)))
# alias(m5)
# # sapply(
#   df_speech[, c("mean_score", "dem", "house_maj",
#                 "minority", "seniority_rs", "year","year_any_na")],
#   function(x) sum(is.na(x))
# )
# 
# df_speech_full <- df_speech %>%
#   filter(!is.na(year))
# 
# head(df_speech_full)
# 
# 
# head(df_speech_full$minority)
# 
# # Run models
# m1 <- lm(mean_score ~ dem + house_maj + minority + factor(year),
#          data = df_speech_full)
# 
# m2 <- lm(mean_score ~ dem + house_maj + minority +
#            seniority_rs + factor(year),
#          data = df_speech_full)
# 
# m3 <- lm(mean_score ~ dem * seniority_rs +
#            house_maj + minority + factor(year),
#          data = df_speech_full)
# 
# 
# 
# # Coefficients to display (exclude year FE and intercept)
# keep_order <- c(
#   "dem",
#   "house_maj",
#   "minority",
#   "seniority_rs",
#   "dem:seniority_rs"
# )
# 
# cov_labels <- c(
#   "Member's Party (D)",
#   "House Majority",
#   "Minority Status",
#   "Member Seniority (Rescaled)",
#   "Member's Party (D) × Seniority (Rescaled)"
# )
# 
# stargazer(
#   m1, m2, m3,
#   type = "latex",
#   dep.var.labels = "Empirical Score",
#   title = "Regression Results",
#   label = "tab:regression_results",
#   align = TRUE,
#   no.space = TRUE,
#   digits = 3,
#   star.cutoffs = c(0.05, 0.01, 0.001),
# 
#   # Show only substantive covariates
#   keep = keep_order,
#   order = keep_order,
#   covariate.labels = cov_labels,
# 
#   # Omit year FE coefficients
#   omit = "factor\\(year\\)",
# 
#   # Remove default summary rows (we add our own)
#   omit.stat = c("f", "ser", "n", "rsq", "adj.rsq"),
# 
#   out = file.path(path, "plots", "regression_results_speech_unit.tex"),
# 
#   add.lines = list(
#     c("Year fixed effects", "Yes", "Yes", "Yes"),
#     c("Seniority Control", "No", "Yes", "Yes"),
#     c("Interaction Terms", "No", "No", "Yes"),
#     c("Observations",
#       format(nobs(m1), big.mark = ","),
#       format(nobs(m2), big.mark = ","),
#       format(nobs(m3), big.mark = ",")),
#     c("$R^2$",
#       sprintf("%.3f", summary(m1)$r.squared),
#       sprintf("%.3f", summary(m2)$r.squared),
#       sprintf("%.3f", summary(m3)$r.squared)),
#     c("Adj. $R^2$",
#       sprintf("%.3f", summary(m1)$adj.r.squared),
#       sprintf("%.3f", summary(m2)$adj.r.squared),
#       sprintf("%.3f", summary(m3)$adj.r.squared))
#   ),
# 
#   notes = c(
#     "The unit of analysis is the speech chunk.",
#     "All models include year fixed effects (coefficients omitted).",
#     "House majority is assigned based on congressional control in the year of the hearing.",
#     "OLS estimates with standard errors in parentheses."
#   ),
#   notes.align = "l"
#   # If you want to suppress the default significance legend:
#   # , notes.append = FALSE
# )
# 
# 
# # committee and topic FE
# 
# 
# # Option A: lm() with committee & topic as factor FEs
# m1_fe <- lm(mean_score ~ dem + house_maj + minority +
#               factor(cmt_govtrack) + factor(majortopic2) + factor(year),
#             data = df_speech_full)
# 
# m2_fe <- lm(mean_score ~ dem + house_maj + minority +
#               seniority_rs + factor(cmt_govtrack) + factor(majortopic2) + factor(year),
#             data = df_speech_full)
# 
# m3_fe <- lm(mean_score ~ dem * seniority_rs +
#               house_maj + minority + factor(cmt_govtrack) + factor(majortopic2) + factor(year),
#             data = df_speech_full)
# 
# # Keep reporting only the same substantive covariates (no FE coef rows)
# omit_pattern <- "factor\\(year\\)|factor\\(cmt_govtrack\\)|factor\\(majortopic2\\)"
# 
# stargazer(
#   m1_fe, m2_fe, m3_fe,
#   type = "latex",
#   dep.var.labels = "Empirical Score",
#   title = "Regression Results (committee & topic controls)",
#   label = "tab:regression_results_fe",
#   align = TRUE,
#   no.space = TRUE,
#   digits = 3,
#   star.cutoffs = c(0.05, 0.01, 0.001),
# 
#   keep = keep_order,
#   order = keep_order,
#   covariate.labels = cov_labels,
# 
#   omit = omit_pattern,
# 
#   omit.stat = c("f", "ser", "n", "rsq", "adj.rsq"),
# 
#   out = file.path(path, "plots", "regression_results_speech_unit_fe.tex"),
# 
#   add.lines = list(
#     c("Year fixed effects", "Yes", "Yes", "Yes"),
#     c("Committee fixed effects", "Yes", "Yes", "Yes"),
#     c("Topic fixed effects", "Yes", "Yes", "Yes"),
#     c("Seniority Control", "No", "Yes", "Yes"),
#     c("Interaction Terms", "No", "No", "Yes"),
#     c("Observations",
#       format(nobs(m1_fe), big.mark = ","),
#       format(nobs(m2_fe), big.mark = ","),
#       format(nobs(m3_fe), big.mark = ",")),
#     c("$R^2$",
#       sprintf("%.3f", summary(m1_fe)$r.squared),
#       sprintf("%.3f", summary(m2_fe)$r.squared),
#       sprintf("%.3f", summary(m3_fe)$r.squared)),
#     c("Adj. $R^2$",
#       sprintf("%.3f", summary(m1_fe)$adj.r.squared),
#       sprintf("%.3f", summary(m2_fe)$adj.r.squared),
#       sprintf("%.3f", summary(m3_fe)$adj.r.squared))
#   ),
# 
#   notes = c(
#     "The unit of analysis is the speech chunk.",
#     "All models include year, committee, and topic fixed effects (coefficients omitted).",
#     "House majority is assigned based on congressional control in the year of the hearing.",
#     "OLS estimates with standard errors in parentheses."
#   ),
#   notes.align = "l"
# )
# 
# 


# 
# # hearing as unit of analysis
# 
# 
# 
# 
# 
# 
# mode_nonmissing <- function(x) {
#   x <- x[!is.na(x)]
#   if (length(x) == 0) return(NA_integer_)
#   ux <- unique(x)
#   ux[which.max(tabulate(match(x, ux)))]
# }
# 
# first_nonmissing <- function(x) {
#   x <- x[!is.na(x)]
#   if (length(x) == 0) return(NA)
#   x[[1]]
# }
# 
# df_hearing <- df %>%
#   group_by(packageId) %>%
#   summarise(
#     mean_score = mean(mean_score, na.rm = TRUE),
#     year = mode_nonmissing(as.integer(year)),
# 
#     dem_prop = mean(dem == 1, na.rm = TRUE),
#     minority_prop = mean(minority == 1, na.rm = TRUE),
#     seniority_rs_mean = mean(seniority_rs, na.rm = TRUE),
# 
#     house_maj = first_nonmissing(house_maj),
#     .groups = "drop"
#   )
# 
# 
# 
# # Load control table
# house_control <- read.csv(paste(here(),"data","congress_control.csv",sep="/")) %>%
#   mutate(
#     start_year = as.integer(start_year),
#     end_year   = as.integer(end_year)
#   )
# 
# # Append house majority ONLY where missing
# df_hearing <- df_hearing %>%
#   mutate(year = as.integer(year)) %>%
#   left_join(
#     house_control,
#     by = join_by(year >= start_year, year < end_year)
#   ) %>%
#   mutate(
#     house_maj = ifelse(
#       is.na(house_maj),
#       majority_party,
#       house_maj
#     )
#   ) %>%
#   select(-start_year, -end_year, -congress, -majority_party)
# 
# 
# 
# sapply(
#   df_hearing[, c("mean_score", "dem_prop", "house_maj",
#                 "minority_prop", "seniority_rs_mean", "year")],
#   function(x) sum(is.na(x))
# )
# 
# 
# # Run models
# m1 <- lm(mean_score ~ dem_prop + house_maj + minority_prop + factor(year),
#          data = df_hearing)
# 
# m2 <- lm(mean_score ~ dem_prop + house_maj + minority_prop +
#            seniority_rs_mean + factor(year),
#          data = df_hearing)
# 
# m3 <- lm(mean_score ~ dem_prop * seniority_rs_mean +
#            house_maj + minority_prop + factor(year),
#          data = df_hearing)
# 
# 
# 
# # Display only the main covariates (omit year FE dummies)
# keep_order <- c(
#   "dem_prop",
#   "house_maj",
#   "minority_prop",
#   "seniority_rs_mean",
#   "dem_prop:seniority_rs_mean"
# )
# 
# cov_labels <- c(
#   "Democratic Proportion",
#   "House Majority",
#   "Minority Proportion",
#   "Mean Seniority (Rescaled)",
#   "Democratic Proportion × Mean Seniority (Rescaled)"
# )
# 
# stargazer(
#   m1, m2, m3,
#   type = "latex",
#   dep.var.labels = "Empirical Score",
#   title = "Regression Results",
#   label = "tab:regression_results",
#   align = TRUE,
#   no.space = TRUE,
#   star.cutoffs = c(0.05, 0.01, 0.001),
#   digits = 3,
# 
#   keep = keep_order,
#   order = keep_order,
#   covariate.labels = cov_labels,
# 
#   omit = "factor\\(year\\)",
#   omit.stat = c("f", "ser", "n", "rsq", "adj.rsq"),
# 
#   out = file.path(path, "plots", "regression_results_hearing_unit.tex"),
# 
#   add.lines = list(
#     c("Year fixed effects", "Yes", "Yes", "Yes"),
#     c("Seniority Control", "No", "Yes", "Yes"),
#     c("Interaction Terms", "No", "No", "Yes"),
#     c("Observations",
#       format(nobs(m1), big.mark = ","),
#       format(nobs(m2), big.mark = ","),
#       format(nobs(m3), big.mark = ",")),
#     c("$R^2$",
#       sprintf("%.3f", summary(m1)$r.squared),
#       sprintf("%.3f", summary(m2)$r.squared),
#       sprintf("%.3f", summary(m3)$r.squared)),
#     c("Adj. $R^2$",
#       sprintf("%.3f", summary(m1)$adj.r.squared),
#       sprintf("%.3f", summary(m2)$adj.r.squared),
#       sprintf("%.3f", summary(m3)$adj.r.squared))
#   ),
# 
#   notes = c(
#     "Unit of analysis is the hearing.",
#     "Democratic Proportion and Minority Proportion are computed within each hearing.",
#     "All models include year fixed effects (coefficients omitted) and report OLS estimates with standard errors in parentheses."
#   ),
#   notes.align = "l"
#   # If you want ONLY these notes (and not the default significance legend), uncomment:
#   # , notes.append = FALSE
# )
# 


```


```{r message=FALSE, warning=FALSE, include=FALSE, paged.print=FALSE}


# ---------- Minimal robust manual LaTeX table using explicit rows -------------
# REQUIREMENTS: coef_char and se_char exist and are lists (one element per model),
# each a named character vector containing exactly the keys in 'rows'.
# rows and row_labels are the explicit vectors you showed and must be used.

rows <- c("(Intercept)", "dem", "house_maj_d", "minority", "seniority_rs", "minority:seniority_rs")
row_labels <- c("Intercept", "Party (D)", "House Control (D)", "Minority Status",
                "Seniority", "Seniority \\times Minority")
nmodels <- length(coef_char)  # should be 5

# Basic safety checks
stopifnot(length(coef_char) == length(se_char))
stopifnot(all(sapply(coef_char, function(ch) all(rows %in% names(ch)))))

# Helper: produce a cell (space for blanks)
cell <- function(x) if (is.null(x) || nchar(x) == 0) " " else x

# Build the table lines (only the body; you can keep your header/footer)
lines <- character()
lines <- c(lines, "% Table generated (explicit-rows safe)")
lines <- c(lines, "\\begin{table}[!htbp] \\centering")
lines <- c(lines, "  \\caption{Regression Results}")
lines <- c(lines, "  \\label{tab:main_regressions}")
lines <- c(lines, "  \\begin{tabular}{@{\\extracolsep{5pt}}lD{.}{.}{-3} D{.}{.}{-3} D{.}{.}{-3} D{.}{.}{-3} D{.}{.}{-3}}")
lines <- c(lines, "  \\\\[-1.8ex]\\hline")
lines <- c(lines, "  \\hline \\\\[-1.8ex]")
lines <- c(lines, paste0("  & \\multicolumn{", nmodels, "}{c}{\\textit{Dependent variable:}} \\\\"))
lines <- c(lines, "  \\cline{2-6}")
lines <- c(lines, paste0("  \\\\[-1.8ex] & \\multicolumn{", nmodels, "}{c}{Empirical Score} \\\\"))
lines <- c(lines, paste0("  \\\\[-1.8ex] & ", paste0(sprintf("\\multicolumn{1}{c}{(%d)}", 1:nmodels), collapse = " & "), " \\\\"))
lines <- c(lines, "  \\hline \\\\[-1.8ex]")

# Body: for each requested row, print coef line then se line
for (r in seq_along(rows)) {
  label <- row_labels[r]
  coefs <- vapply(coef_char, function(ch) cell(ch[ rows[r] ]), FUN.VALUE = "")
  ses   <- vapply(se_char,   function(ch) cell(ch[ rows[r] ]), FUN.VALUE = "")
  lines <- c(lines, paste0("  ", label, " & ", paste(coefs, collapse = " & "), " \\\\"))
  lines <- c(lines, paste0("   & ", paste(ses, collapse = " & "), " \\\\"))
}

# Footer lines (kept minimal)
lines <- c(lines, "  \\hline \\\\[-1.8ex]")
lines <- c(lines, "  \\textit{Note:}  & \\multicolumn{5}{l}{$^{*}$p$<$0.05; $^{**}$p$<$0.01; $^{***}$p$<$0.001} \\\\")
lines <- c(lines, "  \\end{tabular}")
lines <- c(lines, "\\end{table}")

writeLines(lines, con = texfile)
cat('Wrote corrected LaTeX table to:', texfile, '\n')
# --------------------------------------------------------------------------

```


```{r message=FALSE, warning=FALSE, include=FALSE, paged.print=FALSE}





# Perform regression 

m1 <- lm(mean_score ~ dem + house_maj + minority + year,data=df)
m2 <- lm(mean_score ~ dem + house_maj + minority + minority_prop + year,data=df)
m3 <- lm(mean_score ~ dem + house_maj + minority + minority_prop + dem*minority_prop+ year,data=df)
m4 <- lm(mean_score ~ dem + house_maj + minority + minority_prop + seniority + year,data=df)
m5 <- lm(mean_score ~ dem + house_maj + minority + minority_prop + seniority + dem*seniority+ year,data=df)



# Custom labels for coefficients
custom_labels <- c(
  "(Intercept)" = "Intercept",
  "year" = "Year",
  "congress_hearings" = "Congress Session",
#  "pres_partyR" = "President's Party (R)",
#  "house_majR" = "House Majority (R)",
  "divided_house" = "Divided House",
  "divided_senate" = "Divided Senate",
  "dem_dw" = "Committtee Ideology (R)",
  "median_floor" = "Polarization of Floor",
  "chair_dw" = "Chair's Ideology (R)",
  "dem" = "Member's Party (D)",
  "minority" = "Minority Status",
  "seniorty" = "Member's Seniority",
  "type_legislative" = "Legislative Hearing",
  "bill" = "Specific Bill Considered",
  "class_const" = "Constitutional Hearing",
  "cmt_govtrackAppropriations" = "Appropriations Committee",
  "cmt_govtrackArmed Services" = "Armed Services Committee",
  "cmt_govtrackBanking and Currency" = "Banking and Currency Committee",
  "cmt_govtrackBudget" = "Budget Committee",
  "cmt_govtrackEducation and Labor" = "Education and Labor Committee",
  "cmt_govtrackForeign Affairs" = "Foreign Affairs Committee",
  "cmt_govtrackGovernment Operations" = "Government Operations Committee",
  "cmt_govtrackHouse Administration" = "House Administration Committee",
  "cmt_govtrackInterior and Insular Affairs" = "Interior and Insular Affairs Committee",
  "cmt_govtrackInterstate and Foreign Commerce" = "Interstate and Foreign Commerce Committee",
  "cmt_govtrackJudiciary" = "Judiciary Committee",
  "cmt_govtrackPublic Works" = "Public Works Committee",
  "cmt_govtrackRules" = "Rules Committee",
  "cmt_govtrackScience and Astronautics" = "Science and Astronautics Committee",
  "cmt_govtrackSmall Business" = "Small Business Committee",
  "cmt_govtrackVeterans' Affairs" = "Veterans' Affairs Committee",
  "cmt_govtrackWays and Means" = "Ways and Means Committee",
  "majortopic2Agriculture" = "Topic: Agriculture",
  "majortopic2Civil Rights" = "Topic: Civil Rights",
  "majortopic2Defense" = "Topic: Defense",
  "majortopic2Domestic Commerce" = "Topic: Domestic Commerce",
  "majortopic2Education" = "Topic: Education",
  "majortopic2Energy" = "Topic: Energy",
  "majortopic2Environment" = "Topic: Environment",
  "majortopic2Foreign Trade" = "Topic: Foreign Trade",
  "majortopic2Government Operations" = "Topic: Government Operations",
  "majortopic2Health" = "Topic: Health",
  "majortopic2Housing" = "Topic: Housing",
  "majortopic2Immigration" = "Topic: Immigration",
  "majortopic2International Affairs" = "Topic: International Affairs",
  "majortopic2Labor" = "Topic: Labor",
  "majortopic2Law and Crime" = "Topic: Law and Crime",
  "majortopic2Macroeconomics" = "Topic: Macroeconomics",
  "majortopic2Public Lands" = "Topic: Public Lands",
  "majortopic2Social Welfare" = "Topic: Social Welfare",
  "majortopic2Technology" = "Topic: Technology",
  "majortopic2Transportation" = "Topic: Transportation"
)


stargazer(
  regression_result,
  type = "latex",  # Output as LaTeX code
  dep.var.labels = "Empirical Score",  # Set new dependent variable name
  covariate.labels = custom_labels,  # Custom labels for covariates
  out = paste(path, "plots", "regression_results.latex", sep = '/'),  # Save to file
  title = "Regression Results",
  label = "tab:regression_results",
  align = TRUE,
  no.space = TRUE,
  star.cutoffs = c(0.05, 0.01, 0.001),  # Significance stars
  digits = 3,
#  omit = c("majortopic2", "cmt_govtrack"),  # Exclude variables from display
  add.lines = list(
    c("Topic Controls", "Yes"),  # Add custom row
    c("Committee Controls", "Yes")  # Add custom row
  )
)


# Extract coefficients and confidence intervals
coefficients <- tidy(regression_result, conf.int = TRUE)

# Map custom labels to the term column and remove the intercept
coefficients <- coefficients %>%
  filter(term != "(Intercept)") %>%  # Remove the intercept
  mutate(term = recode(term, !!!custom_labels)) %>%  # Apply custom labels
  mutate(term = factor(term, levels = rev(term)))  # Reverse the order

coefficients
# Create the plot
coef_plot <- ggplot(coefficients, aes(y = term, x = estimate)) +
  geom_point(size = 3) +
  geom_errorbarh(aes(xmin = conf.low, xmax = conf.high), height = 0.2) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "black") +
  # Add vertical line at x=0
  scale_x_continuous(
    breaks = seq(-0.05, 0.05, by = 0.025),  # Custom x-axis ticks
    limits = c(-0.05, 0.05)                # Ensure axis spans this range
  ) +
  labs(
    y = "Coefficient",
    x = "Estimate") +
  theme_minimal()


# save the plot
ggsave(paste(path,"plots","regression_coef_plot.png",sep = "/"),
  coef_plot, width = 8, height = 8, dpi = 300)



```




# categories

```{r include=FALSE}

combined_df <- combined_df %>%
  mutate(
    empirical_category = ifelse(
      str_trim(empirical_category) %in% c("", "***"), 
      list(0),  # If empty or "***", set to 0
      str_split(empirical_category, " \\*\\*\\*\\*\\* ") # Split by "*****"
    )
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    empirical_category = ifelse(
      str_trim(empirical_category) %in% c("", "***"), 
      list(0),  # If empty or "***", set to 0
      str_split(empirical_category, ",") # Split by ","
    )
  )


unique_values <- combined_df %>%
  pull(empirical_category) %>%  # Extract the transformed variable as a list
  unlist() %>%                # Flatten the list
  unique()       

# Clean unique_values
unique_values <- unique_values %>%
  str_remove("^c\\(") %>%        # Remove "c(" at the start
  str_remove_all("[^a-zA-Z0-9 ]") %>%  # Remove special characters
  str_trim() %>%                 # Trim leading/trailing whitespace
  unique()   

combined_df <- combined_df %>%
  mutate(
    !!!setNames(
      map(unique_values, 
          ~ as.integer(map_lgl(combined_df$empirical_category, function(x) any(.x %in% x)))),
      paste0("category_", unique_values)
    )
  )


category_columns <- grep("^category_", names(combined_df), value = TRUE)
category_columns <- setdiff(category_columns, "category_0")

averaged_df <- combined_df %>%
  group_by(file_name) %>%
  summarise(across(all_of(category_columns), mean, na.rm = TRUE)) 

# Create a long-format DataFrame for plotting
plot_df <- averaged_df %>%
  summarise(across(all_of(category_columns), mean, na.rm = TRUE)) %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = everything(),
    names_to = "category",
    values_to = "average"
  ) %>%
  mutate(category = gsub("^category_", "", category))

# Create the plot
ggplot(plot_df, aes(x = category, y = average)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", fill = "steelblue") +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(
    title = "Average by Category",
    x = "Category",
    y = "Average Value"
  ) +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))



```


```{r include=FALSE}
a <- df %>%
# filter(cmt_govtrack == "Public Works")%>%
# filter(class_const_2==1)%>%
# filter(hearing == "CHRG-105hhrg38711") %>% 
  arrange(hearing,rownumber) %>%
  group_by(hearing) %>%
  mutate(rownumber = row_number()) %>%
  ungroup()


## heatmap
g <- a %>%
  filter(class_const_3==1) %>%
  filter(type_legislative==0) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = rownumber, y = as.factor(hearing), fill = mean_score)) +
  geom_tile() +
  scale_fill_gradient(low = "white", high = "red", name = "Empirical Score") +
  labs(
      title = "Heatmap of Discussion Progression: Constitutional Hearings",
    x = "Progression of Speech (Rows)",
    y = "Hearing"
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
     axis.text.y = element_blank(),  # Remove y-axis text
    axis.ticks.y = element_blank(),  # Remove y-axis ticks
    legend.position = "right"              # Place legend on the right
  )

ggsave(paste0(path,"/plots/","heatmap_const.png"),
  g,
  width = 8,
  height = 6,
  dpi = 300)









### ridgeline plot
create_ridgeline_plot <- function(data, grouping_var, score_var = "mean_score", row_var = "rownumber") {
  library(ggridges)

  # Check if the grouping variable exists in the dataset
  if (!grouping_var %in% colnames(data)) {
    stop(paste("Grouping variable", grouping_var, "does not exist in the dataset."))
  }
  
  # Check if row_var exists, reset if necessary
  if (!row_var %in% colnames(data)) {
    stop(paste("Row variable", row_var, "does not exist in the dataset."))
  }
  
  # Create the ridgeline plot
  g <- ggplot(data, aes_string(x = row_var, y = paste0("as.factor(", grouping_var, ")"), 
                          height = score_var, group = grouping_var, fill = grouping_var)) +
    geom_density_ridges(stat = "identity", scale = 1) +
    scale_fill_viridis_d(name = grouping_var) +  # Dynamic legend label
    labs(
      title = paste("Ridgeline Plot of Discussion Progression by", grouping_var),
      x = "Progression of Speech",
      y = grouping_var
    ) +
    theme_minimal() +
    theme(
      legend.position = "right",          # Legend on the right
      axis.text.y = element_text(size = 10),  # Customize y-axis text
      axis.text.x = element_text(size = 10),  # Customize x-axis text
      axis.title.x = element_text(size = 12), # Customize x-axis label
      axis.title.y = element_text(size = 12)  # Customize y-axis label
    )
  
  ggsave(paste0(path,"/plots/","ridgeline_",grouping_var,".png"),
  g,
  width = 8,
  height = 6,
  dpi = 300)

}

create_ridgeline_plot(a, grouping_var = "type")






a %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = rownumber, y = mean_score)) +
  geom_line(color = "blue") +
  geom_point(size = 1) +
  labs(
    title = "Discussion Progression",
    x = "Row ID (Progression of Speech)",
    y = "Empirical Score"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()


a %>% ggplot(aes(x = rownumber, y = mean_score, color = mean_score)) +
  geom_point(size = 2) +
  scale_color_gradient(low = "blue", high = "red") +
  labs(
    title = "Discussion Progression (Color by Score)",
    x = "Row ID (Progression of Speech)",
    y = "Score"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()
```



# look at committee seniority 
```{r message=FALSE, warning=FALSE, include=FALSE, paged.print=FALSE}
library(effects)
library(sjPlot)
library(interactions)

m <- lm(mean_score~minority*dem,data = df)


interaction_dem <- plot_model(m, type = "int", terms = c("minority", "dem"))

ggsave(
    filename = paste0(path, "/plots/interaction_dem_plot.png"),
    plot = interaction_dem,
    width = 8,
    height = 6,
    dpi = 300
  )


plot_model(
  lm(mean_score~average_freq_ver5*type,data = df),
           type = "int", terms = c("average_freq_ver5", "type"))


# Get the interaction effect
interaction_effect <- effect("minority*dem", m)

# Plot the effect
plot(interaction_effect)





#Look at committee seniority and tenure, capcity

 # "minority"                              
 # [16] "unified"                                "minuni"                                 "partyloyalty"                          
 # [19] "votepct100"                             "votepct_sq100"                          "seniority_rs"                          
 # [22] "seniority_sq_rs"                        "abs_dwnom1_rs"                          "dem"                                   
 # [25] "freshman"                               "female"                                 "chairspeech"                           
 # [28] "rankmemspeech"                          "leader"                                 "salience"                              
 # [31] "salience_rs"                            "polar"                                  "polar_rs"         

m <- lm(mean_score~seniority_rs*minority,data = df)

# Extract data from plot_model
#lot_data <- plot_model(m, type = "int", terms = c("minority", "seniority_rs"), return.data = TRUE)


plot_data <- get_model_data(m, type = "int", terms = c("minority", "seniority_rs"))

# Clean and relabel
plot_data <- plot_data %>%
  mutate(
    group_label = ifelse(group_col == 0, "Majority", "Minority")
  )

# Rebuild plot manually
interaction_senior <- ggplot(plot_data, aes(x = x, y = predicted, color = group_label, linetype = group_label)) +
  geom_line(size = 1) +
  geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = conf.low, ymax = conf.high, fill = group_label), alpha = 0.2, color = NA) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("Majority" = "black", "Minority" = "black")) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("Majority" = "black", "Minority" = "black")) +
  scale_linetype_manual(values = c("Majority" = "solid", "Minority" = "dashed")) +
  labs(
    x = "Seniority",
    y = "Empirical Score",
    color = NULL,
    fill = NULL,
    linetype = NULL
  ) +
  theme_minimal()

ggsave(
    filename = paste0(path, "/plots/interaction_senior.png"),
    plot = interaction_senior,
    width = 8,
    height = 6,
    dpi = 300
  )
```

```{r include=FALSE}
# Define the variables to plot
class_const_vars <- paste0("class_const_", 1:5)

# Loop through each variable to create and save plots
bar_data <- df %>%
  filter(!is.na(cmt_govtrack)) %>%  # Remove rows with missing committees
  group_by(cmt_govtrack, class_const_5) %>%
  summarise(
  hearings_count = n_distinct(hearing),  # Count unique hearings
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  # Ensure all combinations of cmt_govtrack and class_const_3 are present
  complete(cmt_govtrack, class_const_5, fill = list(hearings_count = 0)) %>%
  group_by(cmt_govtrack) %>%
  mutate(total_hearings = sum(hearings_count)) %>%
  arrange(total_hearings) %>%
  mutate(cmt_govtrack = factor(cmt_govtrack, levels = unique(cmt_govtrack)))  # Set factor levels

g <- ggplot(bar_data, aes(x = cmt_govtrack, y = hearings_count, fill = as.factor(class_const_5))) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", position = position_dodge(width = 0.8), width = 0.7) +
  scale_fill_manual(
    values = c("0" = "blue", "1" = "red"),
    labels = c("0" = "Non-Constitutional", "1" = "Constitutional")
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "Hearings by Committees: Constitutional Subject Matter",
    x = "Committees",
    y = "Number of Hearings",
    fill = "Constitutional Subject Matter"
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    legend.title = element_blank(),
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),  # Rotate x-axis labels
    legend.position = "right"                          # Place legend on the right
  ) + scale_x_discrete(limits = unique(bar_data$cmt_govtrack))
  
  # Save each plot with a unique filename
ggsave(
    filename = paste0(path, "/plots/", "class_const_5", "_barplot.png"),
    plot = g,
    width = 8,
    height = 6,
    dpi = 300
  )
```


# speech lag
```{r include=FALSE}
library(ggeffects)
library(lme4)


a <- df %>%
  arrange(hearing, rownumber) %>%
  group_by(hearing) %>%
  mutate(empirical_score = mean_score,
    lag_empirical_score = lag(empirical_score))

a <- a %>%
  mutate(empirical_change = empirical_score - lag_empirical_score)

model <- lmer(empirical_change ~ lag_empirical_score * dem * minority +
                (1 | hearing), data = a)

plot(model)
qqnorm(resid(model))
qqline(resid(model))


plot(ggpredict(model, terms = c("lag_empirical_score", "dem", "minority")))

a %>%
  mutate(dem = as.factor(dem)) 

g <- ggplot(a, aes(x = lag_empirical_score, y = empirical_change)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.5) +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", alpha = 0.2) +
  labs(
       x = "Lagged Empirical Score", y = "Empirical Score Change")

g
ggsave(
    filename = paste0(path, "/plots/","lag_empirical_score.png"),
    plot = g,
    width = 8,
    height = 6,
    dpi = 300
  )

```


```{r include=FALSE}

party_control <- tibble(
  start_year = c(1996, 2007, 2011), 
  end_year = c(2007, 2011, 2017), 
  majority_party = c("R", "D", "R")
)


g <- df %>%
  filter(!is.na(minority)) %>%
  filter(!is.na(majority_party)) %>%
  mutate(
    minority = as.factor(if_else(minority == 1, "Minority", "Majority"))  # Replace 1 with Minority, 0 with Majority
  ) %>%
  group_by(hearing, minority, year, bill) %>%  # Include bill in grouping
  summarise(mean_empirical = mean(mean_score, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop") %>%
  group_by(minority, year, bill) %>%
  summarise(empirical = mean(mean_empirical, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop") %>%
  ggplot(aes(y = empirical, x = year, group = minority)) +
  geom_point(aes(x = year + 0.5, shape = minority), size = 2) +  # Shift points leftward by 0.5
  geom_smooth(aes(x = year + 0.5, linetype = minority), color = "black", se = TRUE) +  # Black lines with SE and line types
  geom_rect(
    data = party_control,  # Use broader ranges for shading
    aes(
      xmin = start_year, xmax = end_year,  # Use start and end year for ranges
      ymin = -Inf, ymax = Inf, 
      fill = majority_party
    ),
    inherit.aes = FALSE,
    alpha = 0.2  # Transparency for shading
  ) +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = custom_ticks) +  # Custom tick marks for years
  scale_shape_manual(
    values = c("Minority" = 17, "Majority" = 16),  # Custom shapes for points
    name = "Committee Control"
  ) +
  scale_linetype_manual(
    values = c("Minority" = "dashed", "Majority" = "solid"),  # Different line types
    name = "Committee Control"
  ) +
  scale_fill_manual(
    values = c("D" = "blue", "R" = "red"),  # Shading for Majority party
    name = "House Control"  # Legend title for shading
  ) +
  labs(
    x = "Year",
    y = "Empirical Score"
  ) +
  facet_wrap(~ bill, labeller = as_labeller(c(`0` = "No Bill", `1` = "Bill on Table"))) +  # Facet based on bill
  theme_minimal()

ggsave(
    filename = paste0(path, "/plots/minority_byspecificbill_plot.png"),
    plot = g,
    width = 8,
    height = 6,
    dpi = 300
  )


# Process data
df_diff <- df %>%
  filter(type_legislative==1) %>%
  filter(!is.na(minority)) %>%
  filter(!is.na(majority_party)) %>%
  mutate(
    minority = as.factor(if_else(minority == 1, "Minority", "Majority"))  # Replace 1 with Minority, 0 with Majority
  ) %>%
  group_by(hearing, minority, year, bill) %>%
  summarise(mean_empirical = mean(mean_score, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop") %>%
  pivot_wider(
    names_from = minority,
    values_from = mean_empirical,
    values_fill = 0  # Fill missing values with 0
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    diff_empirical = Majority - Minority  # Calculate the difference
  ) %>%
  group_by(year, bill) %>%
  summarise(
    mean_diff = mean(diff_empirical, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop"  # Calculate the average difference for each year and bill status
  )

g <- ggplot(df_diff, aes(x = year, y = mean_diff)) +
  geom_point(size = 2) +  # Points without grouping
  geom_smooth(se = TRUE, color = "black", linetype = "solid") +  # Single style for smooth lines
  geom_rect(
    data = party_control,
    aes(
      xmin = start_year, xmax = end_year,
      ymin = -Inf, ymax = Inf, 
      fill = majority_party
    ),
    inherit.aes = FALSE,
    alpha = 0.2
  ) +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = custom_ticks) +
  scale_fill_manual(
    values = c("D" = "blue", "R" = "red"),
    name = "House Control"
  ) +
  labs(
    x = "Year",
    y = "Difference in Empirical Score (Majority - Minority)"
  ) +
  facet_wrap(~ bill, labeller = as_labeller(c(`0` = "No Bill", `1` = "Bill on Table"))) +  # Facet by bill
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    strip.text = element_text(size = 12, face = "bold"),  # Styling for facet labels
    panel.spacing = unit(1, "lines")  # Space between facets
  )

ggsave(
    filename = paste0(path, "/plots/minoritydiff_byspecificbill_plot.png"),
    plot = g,
    width = 8,
    height = 6,
    dpi = 300
  )




g <- df %>%
    mutate(
    majority_party = if_else(demratio > 0.5, "Democrat", "Republican"),  # Determine majority party
    majority_prop = if_else(demratio > 0.5, demratio, 1 - demratio),  # Proportion of the majority
    minority_prop = 1 - majority_prop  # Proportion of the minority
  ) %>%
  filter(!is.na(minority)) %>%
  filter(!is.na(majority_party)) %>%
  mutate(
    minority = as.factor(if_else(minority == 1, "Minority", "Majority"))  # Replace 1 with Minority, 0 with Majority
  ) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = minority_prop, y = mean_score, group = minority, color = minority, linetype = minority)) +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = TRUE) +  # Use linear model for smoother lines
  scale_color_manual(
    values = c("Minority" = "black", "Majority" = "gray"),  # Custom colors for the groups
    name = "House Member Group"  # Legend title for colors
  ) +
  scale_linetype_manual(
    values = c("Minority" = "dashed", "Majority" = "solid"),  # Custom linetypes for the groups
    name = "House Member Group"  # Legend title for linetypes
  ) +
  labs(
    x = "Majority-Minority Control Gap",
    y = "Empirical Score"
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    legend.position = "right",
    legend.title = element_text(size = 12, face = "bold"),
    legend.text = element_text(size = 10)
  )


g
ggsave(
    filename = paste0(here(), "/plots/minority_proportion.png"),
    plot = g,
    width = 8,
    height = 6,
    dpi = 300
  )



g <- df %>%
  filter(!is.na(year)) %>%
  group_by(year) %>%
  summarise(
    minority_prop = mean(minority_prop, na.rm = TRUE),
    minority_prop_sd = ifelse(n() > 1, sd(minority_prop, na.rm = TRUE), 0), # Avoid NA for single observations
    n = n()
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    minority_prop_se = ifelse(n > 1, minority_prop_sd / sqrt(n), 0) # Avoid division by zero
  ) %>% ggplot(aes(x = year, y = minority_prop)) +
  # Background rectangles for party control
  geom_rect(
    data = party_control,
    aes(
      xmin = start_year, xmax = end_year,
      ymin = -Inf, ymax = Inf, 
      fill = majority_party
    ),
    inherit.aes = FALSE,
    alpha = 0.2
  ) +
  scale_fill_manual(
    values = c("D" = "blue", "R" = "red"),
    name = "House Control"
  ) +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = custom_ticks) +
  
  # Points and error bars with dodging for better visibility
  geom_point(aes(x=year+0.5), size = 2) +  
#  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = minority_prop - minority_prop_se, 
#                    ymax = minority_prop + minority_prop_se),
#                width = 0.2, 
#                position = position_dodge(width = 0.3)) + 
  
  # Smoothed trend line
  geom_smooth(aes(x=year+0.5),method = "lm", se = TRUE) +
  labs(
    x = "Year",
    y = "Partisan Power Balance (Minority Proportion)"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()


ggsave(
    filename = paste0(path, "/plots/minority_proportion_overtime.png"),
    plot = g,
    width = 8,
    height = 6,
    dpi = 300
  )


```





## trifecta effects
```{r include=FALSE}



df <- df %>%
  mutate(
    trifecta = ifelse(divided_house == 0 & divided_senate == 0, 1, 0)
  )

df <- df %>%
  mutate(
    dem_in_maj = ifelse(dem == 1 & dem_maj == 1, 1, 0),
    rep_in_maj = ifelse(dem == 0 & dem_maj == 0, 1, 0)
  )

model_party <- feols(
  mean_score ~ trifecta * dem_in_maj + trifecta * rep_in_maj | cmt_govtrack,
  data = df
)


# Extract coefficients
tm <- tidy(model_party, conf.int = TRUE)
vcov_mat <- vcov(model_party)

# Pull coefficients
dem_maj      <- tm$estimate[tm$term == "dem_in_maj"]
dem_maj_tri  <- tm$estimate[tm$term == "trifecta:dem_in_maj"]
rep_maj      <- tm$estimate[tm$term == "rep_in_maj"]
rep_maj_tri  <- tm$estimate[tm$term == "trifecta:rep_in_maj"]

# Compute standard errors of combined effects
se_dem_combined <- sqrt(
  vcov_mat["dem_in_maj", "dem_in_maj"] +
  vcov_mat["trifecta:dem_in_maj", "trifecta:dem_in_maj"] +
  2 * vcov_mat["dem_in_maj", "trifecta:dem_in_maj"]
)

se_rep_combined <- sqrt(
  vcov_mat["rep_in_maj", "rep_in_maj"] +
  vcov_mat["trifecta:rep_in_maj", "trifecta:rep_in_maj"] +
  2 * vcov_mat["rep_in_maj", "trifecta:rep_in_maj"]
)



effects <- tibble(
  condition = c(
    "Dem Maj + Divided",
    "Dem Maj + Trifecta",
    "Rep Maj + Divided",
    "Rep Maj + Trifecta"
  ),
  estimate = c(
    dem_maj,
    dem_maj + dem_maj_tri,
    rep_maj,
    rep_maj + rep_maj_tri
  ),
  se = c(
    tm$std.error[tm$term == "dem_in_maj"],
    se_dem_combined,
    tm$std.error[tm$term == "rep_in_maj"],
    se_rep_combined
  )
) %>%
  mutate(
    conf.low = estimate - 1.96 * se,
    conf.high = estimate + 1.96 * se,
    party = c("Dem", "Dem", "Rep", "Rep"),
    color = c("lightblue", "blue", "pink", "red")
  )




g_tifecta_effects <-  ggplot(effects, aes(x = condition, y = estimate, fill = color)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", width = 0.6, color = "black") +
  # geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = conf.low, ymax = conf.high), width = 0.2) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed") +
  scale_fill_identity() +
  labs(
    title = "Effect of Majority Status on Empirical Discourse by Party & Trifecta",
    y = "Estimated Change in Empirical Score",
    x = NULL
  ) +
  theme_minimal()


ggsave(
    filename = paste0(path, "/plots/trifecta_effects.png"),
    plot = g_tifecta_effects,
    width = 8,
    height = 6,
    dpi = 300
  )

##########################

df_summary <- df %>%
  filter(!is.na(mean_score)) %>%
  mutate(
    speaker_party = ifelse(dem == 1, "Dem", "Rep"),
    context = case_when(
      dem_maj == 1 & trifecta == 1 ~ "Dem Maj + Trifecta",
      dem_maj == 1 & trifecta == 0 ~ "Dem Maj + Divided",
      dem_maj == 0 & trifecta == 1 ~ "Rep Maj + Trifecta",
      dem_maj == 0 & trifecta == 0 ~ "Rep Maj + Divided"
    )
  ) %>%
  filter(!is.na(speaker_party))



group_means <- df_summary %>%
  group_by(speaker_party, context) %>%
  summarise(
    mean_score = mean(mean_score),
    sd_score = sd(mean_score),
    n = n(),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    se = ifelse(is.na(sd_score), 0, sd_score / sqrt(n)),
    ci_low = ifelse(is.na(sd_score), mean_score, mean_score - 1.96 * se),
    ci_high = ifelse(is.na(sd_score), mean_score, mean_score + 1.96 * se)
  )



g_trifecta <- ggplot(group_means, aes(x = context, y = mean_score, fill = speaker_party)) +
  geom_bar(
    stat = "identity",
    position = position_dodge(width = 0.7),
    width = 0.6,
    color = "black"
  ) +
  geom_errorbar(
    aes(ymin = ci_low, ymax = ci_high),
    position = position_dodge(width = 0.7),  # 👈 same dodge!
    width = 0.2
  ) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("Dem" = "blue", "Rep" = "red")) +
  labs(
    title = "Raw Mean Empirical Score by Party & Institutional Context",
    x = NULL,
    y = "Empirical Score"
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 30, hjust = 1))


ggsave(
    filename = paste0(path, "/plots/trifecta_raw.png"),
    plot = g_trifecta,
    width = 8,
    height = 6,
    dpi = 300
  )


```
